annotations.label: Separable space

anchor.snippet: a topological space is called separable if it contains a countable dense subset

annotations.note: Class `SeparableSpace` in Mathlib.Topology.Bases states existence of a countable dense subset.

annotations.label: Continuous functions determined by dense subset

anchor.snippet: every continuous function on a separable space whose image is a subset of a Hausdorff space is determined by its values on the countable dense subset

annotations.note: `Continuous.ext_on` proves two continuous maps into a T2 space agree everywhere if they agree on a dense set — generalizes the countable-dense version.

annotations.label: Finite or countable spaces are separable

anchor.snippet: Any topological space that is itself finite or countably infinite is separable

annotations.note: Instance `Countable.to_separableSpace` gives `SeparableSpace α` from `[Countable α]`.

annotations.label: Real line is separable

anchor.snippet: the real line , in which the rational numbers form a countable dense subset

annotations.note: No standalone `SeparableSpace ℝ` instance exists, but it is inferable from `Rat.denseRange_cast` via `SeparableSpace.of_denseRange` (also derivable from `SecondCountableTopology ℝ`).

annotations.label: Euclidean space is separable

anchor.snippet: -dimensional Euclidean space is separable

annotations.note: No direct `SeparableSpace (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n))` instance, but derivable via second-countability of Euclidean space and `SecondCountableTopology.to_separableSpace`.

annotations.label: Uncountable discrete space is not separable

anchor.snippet: A simple example of a space that is not separable is a discrete space of uncountable cardinality

annotations.note: `separableSpace_iff_countable` for `[DiscreteTopology α]` gives `SeparableSpace α ↔ Countable α`, so uncountable discrete is not separable.

annotations.label: Second-countable implies separable

anchor.snippet: Any second-countable space is separable

annotations.note: Priority-100 instance `SecondCountableTopology.to_separableSpace` gives exactly this.

annotations.label: Metrizable: separable iff second countable iff Lindelöf

anchor.snippet: a metrizable space is separable if and only if it is second countable, which is the case if and only if it is Lindelöf

annotations.note: The Lindelöf→second-countable direction for (pseudo)metrizable spaces is available, and separable metric→second-countable is `UniformSpace.secondCountable_of_separable`; the full three-way biconditional is not packaged as a single lemma.

annotations.label: Continuous image of separable is separable

anchor.snippet: Any continuous image of a separable space is separable

annotations.note: `isSeparable_range` (and `DenseRange.separableSpace`) proves the continuous image of a separable space is separable.

annotations.label: Product of at most continuum many separable spaces is separable

anchor.snippet: A product of at most continuum many separable spaces is separable

annotations.note: Mathlib only proves separability of countable products (Hewitt–Marczewski–Pondiczery's continuum-many strengthening not present).

annotations.label: Separable but not second countable

anchor.snippet: We can construct an example of a separable topological space that is not second countable

annotations.note: No explicit example construction found showing separable but not second-countable.

annotations.label: Trivial topology is separable

anchor.snippet: any set endowed with the trivial topology is separable, as well as second countable, quasi-compact , and connected

annotations.note: No lemma stating that the indiscrete/`⊥`-topology is separable was found.

annotations.label: First-countable separable Hausdorff has continuum cardinality bound

anchor.snippet: A first-countable , separable Hausdorff space (in particular, a separable metric space) has at most the continuum cardinality

annotations.note: Cardinality bound `#X ≤ 𝔠` for first-countable separable Hausdorff spaces not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separable Hausdorff cardinality bound

anchor.snippet: A separable Hausdorff space has cardinality at most

annotations.note: The `2^𝔠` cardinality bound for separable Hausdorff spaces is not present.

annotations.label: General Hausdorff dense-subset cardinality bound

anchor.snippet: The same arguments establish a more general result

annotations.note: General Hausdorff cardinality bound in terms of density character not found.

annotations.label: Hewitt–Marczewski–Pondiczery theorem

anchor.snippet: Hewitt–Marczewski–Pondiczery theorem

annotations.note: No trace of Hewitt–Marczewski–Pondiczery in Mathlib; only countable-index products of separable spaces are handled.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem (constructive context)

anchor.snippet: A famous example of a theorem of this sort is the Hahn–Banach theorem

annotations.note: Kind moderated from 'example' to 'theorem' — Hahn–Banach is a named theorem.

annotations.label: Compact metric space is separable

anchor.snippet: Every compact metric space (or metrizable space) is separable

annotations.note: Kind moderated from 'example' to 'proposition' — this is a general statement, not an example.
annotations.note_extra: `IsCompact.isSeparable` proves every compact set in a pseudometrizable space is separable.

annotations.label: Countable union of separable subspaces is separable

anchor.snippet: Any topological space that is the union of a countable number of separable subspaces is separable

annotations.note: `IsSeparable.iUnion` proves that a countable union of separable subsets is separable.

annotations.label: C(K,ℝ) is separable

anchor.snippet: of all continuous functions from a compact subset

annotations.note: Instance provides `SeparableSpace C(X,Y)` when X is second-countable locally compact and Y is second-countable — covers `C(K,ℝ)` with K compact metric.

annotations.label: Lebesgue spaces are separable

anchor.snippet: The Lebesgue spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `Lp E p μ` has second-countable (hence separable) topology when the measure is separable and E is separable; not a bare `SeparableSpace (Lᵖ)` instance.

annotations.label: C[0,1] is separable

anchor.snippet: of continuous real-valued functions on the unit interval

annotations.note: Direct consequence of the general `ContinuousMap.instSeparableSpace` since [0,1] is second-countable locally compact.

annotations.label: Banach–Mazur theorem

anchor.snippet: The Banach–Mazur theorem asserts that any separable Banach space is isometrically isomorphic to a closed linear subspace of

annotations.note: No Banach–Mazur embedding of separable Banach spaces into C[0,1] found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert space separable iff countable orthonormal basis

anchor.snippet: A Hilbert space is separable if and only if it has a countable orthonormal basis

annotations.note: Mathlib has `exists_hilbertBasis` (arbitrary index), but no biconditional linking separability with countability of the Hilbert basis.

annotations.label: Separable infinite-dim Hilbert isometric to ℓ²

anchor.snippet: any separable, infinite-dimensional Hilbert space is isometric to the space

annotations.note: `HilbertBasis.repr` gives an isometry to `lp (fun _ => 𝕜) 2` for an arbitrary index, but a specialized statement for separable infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces isometric to ℓ²(ℕ) is not present.

annotations.label: Sorgenfrey line is separable not second-countable

anchor.snippet: An example of a separable space that is not second-countable is the Sorgenfrey line

annotations.note: No Sorgenfrey line construction found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separable σ-algebra

anchor.snippet: A separable σ-algebra is a σ-algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has `CountablyGenerated` σ-algebras and `MeasureTheory.IsSeparable` for measures, but not the classical pseudometric-based `separable σ-algebra` definition.

annotations.label: First uncountable ordinal not separable

anchor.snippet: The first uncountable ordinal

annotations.note: Non-separability of ω₁ with the order topology is not formalized.

annotations.label: ℓ^∞ bounded sequences not separable

anchor.snippet: The Banach space

annotations.note: `lp E ∞` exists in Mathlib but no lemma stating it is non-separable.

annotations.label: Bounded variation functions not separable

anchor.snippet: The Banach space of functions of bounded variation is not separable

annotations.note: No BV-as-Banach-space non-separability result found.

annotations.label: Subspaces of separable spaces

anchor.snippet: A subspace of a separable space need not be separable

annotations.note: No counterexample formalized showing a subspace of a separable space need not be separable.

annotations.label: Open subspace of separable is separable

anchor.snippet: every open subspace of a separable space is separable

annotations.note: `IsOpenEmbedding.separableSpace` yields separability of the domain from separability of the codomain via an open embedding — applies to open subspaces.

annotations.label: Every subspace of a separable metric space is separable

anchor.snippet: every subspace of a separable metric space is separable

annotations.note: In pseudometrizable spaces `IsSeparable.separableSpace` upgrades an `IsSeparable` set to a `SeparableSpace` subtype, so every subset of a separable metric space is separable.

annotations.label: Every space embeds in separable space of same cardinality

anchor.snippet: every topological space is a subspace of a separable space of the same cardinality

annotations.note: No embedding result of an arbitrary space into a separable space of the same cardinality.

annotations.label: Cardinality of C(X) on separable X

anchor.snippet: The set of all real-valued continuous functions on a separable space has a cardinality equal to

annotations.note: Cardinality bound on C(X,ℝ) for separable X not found.

annotations.label: Separable with uncountable closed discrete subspace not normal

anchor.snippet: If X is a separable space having an uncountable closed discrete subspace, then X cannot be normal

annotations.note: `IsClosed.mk_lt_continuum` (module `Topology.Separation.NotNormal`) shows a closed discrete subset of a separable normal space has cardinality < continuum, contradicting uncountability of size ≥ 𝔠 — this is the Jones lemma content.

annotations.label: Equivalences for compact Hausdorff X

anchor.snippet: For a compact Hausdorff space X , the following are equivalent

annotations.note: The compact-Hausdorff equivalence package (separable/metrizable/second-countable via continuous functions) is not present as a single theorem.

annotations.label: Separable metric embeds in Hilbert cube

anchor.snippet: Every separable metric space is homeomorphic to a subset of the Hilbert cube

annotations.note: `exists_embedding_to_hilbert_cube` for `[MetricSpace X] [SeparableSpace X]` gives an embedding into `ℕ → I`.

annotations.label: Fréchet embedding into ℓ^∞

anchor.snippet: this is known as the Fréchet embedding

annotations.note: The Kuratowski (a.k.a. Fréchet) embedding gives an isometric embedding of any separable metric space into ℓ^∞(ℕ,ℝ).

annotations.label: Banach embedding into C([0,1])

anchor.snippet: Every separable metric space is isometric to a subset of C([0,1])

annotations.note: No isometric embedding of separable metric spaces into C([0,1]) found.

annotations.label: Embedding into Urysohn universal space

anchor.snippet: Every separable metric space is isometric to a subset of the Urysohn universal space

annotations.note: The Urysohn universal metric space is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonseparable density-α embedding

anchor.snippet: A metric space of density equal to an infinite cardinal α is isometric to a subspace of

annotations.note: Density character and its associated ℓ^∞(α) embedding are not formalized in Mathlib.
